samples:
I'm gonna point this right at you
And shoot it just like this
You know that I could not sleep last night
Thinking of what might have been amiss
What if my patience has been tested?
Did we not both decide to trust in me?
Then where have all these doubts begun
When you did nothing but give me peace


We may be standing here, eye to eye
But one is a body and one is a soul
Don't you judge your brother by his cover
Just because he wouldn't let you go
Let the past stay buried in our past
Trust in reason when your blood is high


Some may think they see in me only
The sum total of their fears
So there's a voice in me telling me
Something different to them all
Why can't they not see through this lens
Through which they are choosing to see me
They're projecting everything on me


It was always time enough for someone else
It was always someone else who called "uncle"
But now it seems as though everyone is getting wise
In this place of solace known as home
But they won't even speak
A word to save themselves
All they seem to want from me
Is some miracle to save the day

Well, I suppose I oughta' listen
To what they've got to say
If I wanna keep my place in this
Safe and sane society
But I cannot abide the thought of keeping
Still while others run before me
Must I sit still here while the world around me
Drags itself along with me
Like a stone tied to my ankle
Or a noose hung 'round my neck?
I must change this or die trying
And I will either way



Maybe Jesus isn't coming down tonight
Maybe Satan ain't hiding out in the light
Maybe faith in something other than yourself
Might have been a little piece of Eden left to hold us up
Heal me
Keep me safe
Help me rise above myself
Please
Open my eyes so I may see

My mind's on a different frequency
So I might not see eye to eye with you today


I hear voices calling me away from you, oh
They want me to get out of your way, now


You better watch yourself around him
He don't even like his daddy much
He just likes his money and what he buys it with
And the son's gotta keep it down, he has enough
Son's got problems that must come in handy
His daddy seems content to doze
But if only they could both learn to compromise
Then maybe they wouldn't be so numb


Maybe there was something we could have said
We didn't say it well enough
Well enough to save some ground before we lost our heads

Watch your back my brother
Don't let your guard down while around him


You better watch yourself around him
He don't even like his daddy much
He just likes his money and what he buys it with
And the son's gotta keep it down, he has enough
Son's got problems that must come in handy
His daddy seems content to doze
But if only they could both learn to compromise
Then maybe they would both stop using me as an excuse
I feel used and abused when I am sober or high


Something's gonna happen soon
The father will point the finger at the son
Who then will surely blame it all upon me
If I were you I'd slip a little water in, yeah
It's getting cold and winter is approaching
It’s time for this one to go

I was told once that I would change the world,
So many times and in so many ways.
I must have been in a place or in a state of grace,
To even think something like that could come my way.
And then some light within me started to fade away.


When did we become so scared of our own shadow?
How can we be paralyzed by what might happen?
The time has come for us to trust ourselves instead.
Trust yourself enough to use your mind to make a difference.


If you're bored with all this talk, there's a door at the end of this hallway
It doesn't look like much, but it sure beats sitting around moping 'round while
You watch your life slip away from you. Trust yourself enough to make a difference.


There is no one who can save you. It is time to rise up now and
Save yourself. The time has come to take control, don't judge,
Just do it. Trust yourself enough to make a difference.

Change the past, forgive yourself, forgive others
Open wide, breathe them out, let go, start anew

Some may call me strange, some may label me an addict
But I know better than they'll ever know just how lucky I am
Don't you wanna change things? Then why are you still here?
We need people who won't sit still, so move, rise above this festering swamp

I'm not a liar, I don't wanna lie
So give it up to me and let the tension come alive


Settle down and hush now as we begin
The beginning of something that has never been
Just as quick as lightning comes through my window
It just so happens to come from you

Oh-oh, oh-oh


If I could speak then maybe I would whisper
You are the only one who has ever mattered
Take it all in, feel the tension rising
Gonna hold on, gonna hang on for a while


We will raise our voices high above the rushing tide
Shout some words or shout a warning before they might be too
Late, late to say goodbye
Hush your heart and listen to the wind whistle past
There is nothing more you can do but what you're trying to do


If I could speak then maybe I would whisper
You are the only one who has ever mattered
Take it all in, feel the tension rising
Gonna hold on, gonna hang on for a while


No place like home no, no place like home


Like a pillar of salt, like a tower made of sand
That once held firm until someone came along
And rocked the boat with waves of memory calling out
"Hey! Wake up!" and "Hey! Come back"! Hey! Wake up!



Wake up now, wake up now, time to go
Time to get up off this cold ocean floor
Riddle my head with questions
Breathe in deep, breathe out loud
Swim away from these memories 'til it hurts
Swim away, swim away

They say, “Get up, get up now”
This is the beginning of something new
You are the way and you are the one
Who can save our lives in a world gone numb
The light won't come through until we rise as one


When I said what I meant
I told all those lies that they wanted to hear from me
Then I opened my eyes to see them as they are
And it hurt like hell before I saw the light in you


There will be no light if you don’t rise
No salvation if you sit back down
Don’t you know the time? It's time to open your eyes
Don't you know the time? It's time for you to rise


So then what am I to say when someone comes up
To point fingers at me with an attitude?
Well, the finger was pointed right at me anyway
Pointing right at me like there's nothing better I could've done
Like there's nothing better I could've done

They point their fingers at me with an attitude
Calling me a liar 'cause I have to listen to you
Why don't they not speak? Why don't they just disappear?
It hurts like hell when people lie like this
Pointing their fingers like this






Said, "What am I gonna do when someone says, 'Hey, fuckin'
Fucking Bette Davis eye shadow on your ass?"
He has another mirror so why don't we burn his house down?
We gotta cleanse ourselves if we wanna start anew

Now, lookin' right through ya, he says, "Ah, fuckin' Bette Davis eye shadow on your ass"
How would any one dare tell you how to treat me?
All but some motherfuckers who really hate you
Oh, it sure gets old to keep hearing these lame jokes
These guys think they got the rights to treat us like hoes
Fuck yourself, motherfucker, you piece of shit, I hope
Your fucking dick sucks monkey balls 'cause you suck donkey dicks




Suck donkey dicks

I've been watching you
Keep your distance while you whisper to me, "You suck"


The light is fading as we speak and I can feel it slipping away
Suck it up, keep it down or I will see this through one of these days
And if there's any evidence left in the wreckage that remains
Well it won't be long before I'm free of all this hopeless tension
This whole situation has become a problem that you have to deal with
No hope no way out there for us now but this black hole right here




Take it easy now don't go pushing so hard on my buttons

I know what it's like when you're staring into nothing at all
It doesn't hurt any less when someone tells you how much they love you
And it doesn't help anything any more if you could only let something go
Something other than me and this hopeless place where we both seem to be stuck
Try to remember when we were high above this hellish ground below
High enough not to notice anything, anywhere, anything


I'll take it easy now, don't push me so hard, I'll bite your head off

You know better than me
What's what and why
I might not be a prodigy
But I can write a rhyme


In the shade of this old willow tree
Weepin' as she watches you go
If I could only comfort you
Then maybe there would be hope in so


My little angel, don't you cry
Don't you cry or hold your head down low
The sun will soon shine through those clouds
And then we'll see him back to home


Well, now don't you worry, baby
This won't hurt you any
When I pull these stones away
They're like you anyway
So just put your faith in me
Just let me handle this one


She looked at me with eyes full of sorrow
As if she knew what lay ahead
Why don't you both not leave the valley?
And take your place within my circle of dependants


My little angel, don't you cry
Don't you cry or hold your head down low
The sun will soon shine through those clouds
And then we'll see him back to home

Fade
I've been thinking about you since the night we met
Tried to keep a cool head but my heart wouldn't let
You are the only one that I have ever wanted, and will


Fade



Fade




Been thinkin' 'bout you since the night we met
Tried to keep a cool head but my heart won't let
You are the only one that I've ever wanted, and will



Fade




It's almost like I need you to breathe in the air
And just before you leave me hold me out of fear
I don't want to see you go
Don't want to see you go
Don't want to see you go
Don't want to see you go
Oh oh oh oh oh oh


Fade




They told me it was over when we said goodbye
The damage had been done; I did not even cry
We didn't even start this thing
So how could it end?
What happened to our trust in all?


If I might begin at the beginning then
All time would seem to be an empty place
A barren desert, where nothing grows except weeds
But maybe soon enough something will come over me
Like a shadow or a whisper from a stranger
And suddenly everything will change again
Like a shadow or a whisper from a stranger


When you are gone, there is no sun, they tell me
Why can't you stay with me? Don't you know that I-I-I-I
Will die without you now? Why can't you stay with me?

Drank too much
Danced too long
And it didn't hurt no one but me
Slipped away like a snake on a ladder
Lived the way I wanted to


I could have been somebody
I could have been anybody
You could have been anything, you know?
Don't go pointin' your finger at me
Trust enough to keep holding me up


If I ever get my soul back
I hope I never see your face again
Tried so hard not to judge ya
Now that I know better, oh well
But if I could see your face tomorrow
All I would want is just to kick your ass again
So why don't we take this outside?
Why don't we settle this once and 4е for all?


Get up, out of your head now
Come down, give it to me raw
Just say you are willing
Then do what I'm telling you to
(Drank too much)
(Danced too long)
(It didn't hurt no one but me)
Yeah (Well, how about something else?)

What are we to say? What are we to do?
To console and comfort the ones we've been told of?
We're helpless, hopeless, we'll never see them again


They took one piece from me, they took a part of me
And I was just a step away from holding you


I have had enough, no one speaks for me
My will has long since died and I am filled with rage
But I must wait until the time is right
The tide that comes to bring our lives back home


It's not enough, it wasn't enough then, it isn't now
Your voice so young and innocent can't heal this wound
You could've spoken any other way than how you did
Any other way but this, but it's always like this


There's nothing left to say but what we didn't want to hear
So help us understand why we were wrong when we spoke these words
This separation, this distance between them hurts
This divide between me and you hurts
This separation hurts
